Name: Radial Color Filter
Description: Applies a stylized color filtering effect, typically using a radial gradient to simulate atmospheric distortion, heat haze, or artistic overlays. The effect blends between two colors based on screen-space distance from a defined center point.

Fields.Name: Enable Color Paraffin Filter
Fields.Description: Enables or disables the color paraffin filter effect. When disabled, no additional color overlay is applied.

Fields.Name: Filter Mode
Fields.Description: Selects the blending or application mode of the filter (e.g., additive, multiplicative, overlay, or screen-space modulation). Determines how the effect interacts with the underlying image.

Fields.Name: Radiation: Center Position
Fields.Description: Screen-space position (typically normalized coordinates) defining the center of the radial effect.

Fields.Name: Radiation: Radius
Fields.Description: Radius of the radial effect from the center point. Determines how far the color transition extends across the screen.

Fields.Name: Radiation: Start Color
Fields.Description: Color applied at the center of the effect. Typically represents the strongest influence of the filter.

Fields.Name: Radiation: End Color
Fields.Description: Color at the outer edge of the effect radius. Defines how the filter fades out toward the screen edges.

Fields.Name: Radiation: Curvature
Fields.Description: Controls the falloff curve of the radial gradient. Higher values produce a sharper transition, while lower values create a smoother gradient.
